title: HST WFC3/IR Up-the-Ramp Linearity Audit
question: How does count-rate linearity vary with accumulated fluence, quadrant and data-quality flags in selected WFC3/IR MULTIACCUM exposures?
gap: An archive-level verification; not a replacement for calwf3 or a new calibration reference file.
dataMode: real public HST WFC3/IR IMA products
novelty: An archive-level verification exercise; not a replacement for calwf3 or a new nonlinearity calibration reference file.
methodology: Accumulated counts vs. SAMPTIME are extracted per pixel from each MULTIACCUM IMA file's non-destructive reads. A weighted/robust linear fit to the early (low-fluence) reads establishes the expected linear rate; residuals of all reads from that fit reveal growing nonlinearity at high fluence. A separate rate+curvature quadratic fit (internally parameter-normalized to avoid ill-conditioning) estimates the nonlinearity coefficient directly. Data-quality bits (verified against the real spacetelescope/hstcal source) exclude cosmic-ray/saturated/rejected reads from the fits, with exclusion counts reported rather than silently applied. Results are reported per readout quadrant and binned by accumulated fluence.
assumptions: IMA files follow the standard WFC3 MULTIACCUM convention: one (SCI, ERR, DQ, SAMP, TIME) extension quintet per read, EXTVER=1 is the last (deepest) read; ima_io.py raises DataSchemaError rather than assuming this silently if absent.

Single-pixel apertures (aperture_radius=0) are used by default; aperture summing rescales the fitted curvature coefficient by approximately 1/N_pixels and is documented as a known limitation rather than corrected for.

WFC3/IR data-quality bit definitions (HOTPIX=16, SPIKE=1024, SATPIXEL=256, DATAREJECT=8192, HIGH_CURVATURE=16384, etc.) were verified directly against the real spacetelescope/hstcal source rather than assumed from memory.
limitations: An archive-level verification exercise, not a replacement for calwf3 or a new nonlinearity calibration reference file.
Aperture-summed curvature values are not directly comparable to single-pixel curvature values without rescaling by approximately 1/N_pixels.

The Baggett (2019) WFC3 instrument review citation in the source project pack could not be independently verified (DOI does not resolve); retained with a TODO_VERIFY note rather than deleted or re-invented, alongside a verified, on-topic replacement (arXiv:2602.12110).
reports/report.tex could not be compiled to PDF in this environment (no local LaTeX toolchain); only structural completeness was verified.

citation.author: Biswajit Jana
